note: v3 — added 4 SolidWorks Model Mania parts on top of v2 (22 NIST + 10 seed). Model Mania parts are envelope-only briefs (no drawings) at hard difficulty. Seed tier is for plumbing/baseline and should NOT be averaged into scoreboard composite.

briefs.brief_text: Build a rectangular plate 40 mm long × 30 mm wide × 20 mm thick. Place the corner at the origin with the long side along X.
Build a 25 mm cube with one corner at the origin.
Build a cylinder 20 mm in diameter and 30 mm tall. Axis along the world Z, base on the XY plane centered on the origin.
Mounting plate 60 mm × 40 mm × 5 mm thick, with a single 8 mm diameter through-hole in the center. Plate corner at the origin, long side along X, hole axis along Z.
Mounting plate 80 mm × 60 mm × 6 mm thick, with four 4 mm diameter through-holes, one near each corner, 10 mm in from each edge. Plate corner at the origin.
Washer: outer diameter 30 mm, inner diameter 15 mm, thickness 3 mm. Axis along world Z, base on the XY plane centered on origin.
Threaded-style standoff cylinder: outer diameter 10 mm, inner bore 4 mm through, total height 25 mm. Axis along world Z, base on the XY plane centered on origin.
L-bracket: two legs, each 50 mm long and 30 mm wide, joined at a right angle. Leg thickness 5 mm. One leg lies flat on the XY plane; the other rises along Z.

Flat strap 60 mm long × 20 mm wide × 4 mm thick with a single centered slot along the length: slot is 40 mm long × 8 mm wide with fully-rounded ends (slot is a rectangle capped with two semicircles). Strap corner at the origin.
Rectangular plate 50 mm × 50 mm × 10 mm thick with a 12 mm diameter through-hole positioned 15 mm in from the +X edge and 15 mm in from the +Y edge (i.e., not centered). Plate corner at the origin.

Build the mechanical part specified by the attached engineering drawing sheet. The drawing uses ASME Y14 conventions: read the orthographic views, dimensions, tolerances, and any GD&T feature-control frames carefully. Match every dimension and feature placement. (CTC 01 – complex plate with through-holes, counterbore, and hex pocket)
briefs.notes: Engineering drawing with GD&T, tolerances, feature-control frames. Volume L4/L5 grading should tolerate ±5% since the agent may interpret nominal dimensions within tolerance.

briefs.brief_text: Build the mechanical part shown in the reference isometric render. The part fits inside an approximately 800 × 450 × 150 mm axis-aligned bounding box. Match the shape and feature layout as closely as possible — exact dimensions are at your discretion, but proportions and feature placement should match the render. (CTC 01 – complex plate with through-holes, counterbore, and hex pocket)
briefs.notes: Same NIST part as the _drawing variant, but the brief only gives the iso render + bbox envelope. Tests shape replication without dimensional rigor. L4/L5 thresholds should be relaxed since 'match the shape' invites proportional scaling.

briefs.brief_text: Build the mechanical part shown in the reference isometric render. The part fits inside an approximately 61 × 70 × 168 mm axis-aligned bounding box. This is a SolidWorks Model Mania competition part — expect compound features, thoughtful dimensional relationships, and non-obvious interactions between geometry. Match the shape and feature layout as closely as possible. (SolidWorks Model Mania 2009 – Phase 1)
briefs.notes: SolidWorks Model Mania competition Phase 1 part. Non-obvious compound geometry intended to challenge skilled human CAD users under time pressure. No accompanying drawing — evaluate via shape replication against the STEP ground truth.

briefs.brief_text: Build the mechanical part specified by the attached SolidWorks Model Mania drawing sheet. Read the orthographic views, dimensions, and any tolerances carefully; the drawing is the ground truth for geometry. Model Mania competition parts are designed to be non-obvious under time pressure — watch for compound features whose dimensional interactions matter. (SolidWorks Model Mania 2009 – Phase 1)
briefs.notes: SolidWorks Model Mania problem drawing sourced from the official archive. Dimensional rigor expected — match the drawing, not just the overall shape.
